data.text: I'll start by exploring the relevant parts of the codebase to understand what exists before implementing the new HTTP routes and MCP tools.

input.description: Explore server routes, MCP tools, and agent CLI commands
input.prompt:
Explore the DDx repository at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-4c9f51f9-20260429T192729-eeef7323 to understand:

1. The existing HTTP server routes - look in cli/internal/server/ for route registration and handlers
2. The existing MCP tools - look in cli/internal/mcp/ for tool definitions
3. The agent CLI commands for models, catalog, and capabilities - look in cli/cmd/ for agent-related commands
4. How the agent models/catalog/capabilities data is structured - what functions/types exist

Please read the key files and report:
- The pattern for adding HTTP routes (how routes are registered, handler structure)
- The pattern for adding MCP tools (how tools are defined and registered)
- What data the CLI commands `ddx agent models`, `ddx agent catalog show`, `ddx agent capabilities`, `ddx agent usage` return
- Any existing types/structs for model catalog data
- File paths for: server route registration, MCP tool registration, agent command implementations

Be thorough and read the actual file contents for the key files.

30	// Routing test seams (ddx-755f5881 AC #2): record every RouteRequest issued
31	// by the default `ddx agent run` path so unit tests can assert that the
32	// default path calls ResolveRoute exactly once with Profile: "default" and
33	// no other fields. Production code never reads this slice; it is exported
34	// only for tests.
35	var (
36		routeRequestRecorderMu sync.Mutex
37		routeRequestRecorder   []agentlib.RouteRequest
38	)
39	
40	func recordRouteRequestForTest(req agentlib.RouteRequest) {
41		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
42		routeRequestRecorder = append(routeRequestRecorder, req)
43		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
44	}
45	
46	// RecordedRouteRequestsForTest returns a copy of all RouteRequests recorded
47	// by the agent CLI surface since the last reset.
48	func RecordedRouteRequestsForTest() []agentlib.RouteRequest {
49		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
50		defer routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
51		out := make([]agentlib.RouteRequest, len(routeRequestRecorder))
52		copy(out, routeRequestRecorder)
53		return out
54	}
55	
56	// ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest clears the route-request recording slice.
57	func ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest() {
58		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
59		routeRequestRecorder = nil
60		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
61	}

42	// serverPromptCapBytes caps the size of inline prompt bodies accepted on
43	// server-side ingress (POST /api/agent/run). FEAT-022 §9 / Stage D2. It is
44	// a package-level variable rather than a literal so tests can lower the
45	// cap without writing multi-MB request bodies. Production callers must not
46	// mutate it.
47	var serverPromptCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes
48	
49	// serverInlineCapBytes caps inline content bodies returned on egress paths
50	// — MCP tool responses and REST JSON responses that carry library-doc,
51	// execution-bundle, diff, session, or persona text. FEAT-022 §10 / §11.
52	// Stage E1. Package-level variable so tests can lower the cap; production
53	// callers must not mutate it.
54	var serverInlineCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxInlinedFileBytes
55	
56	// mcpText constructs a text-typed mcpContent whose Text body is bounded by
57	// serverInlineCapBytes via evidence.ClampOutput. The Truncated and
58	// OriginalBytes fields are always populated (false/len(text) when the body
59	// fits within the cap). This is the single mcpContent{Type:"text"} literal
60	// site in this package — every other caller must route through this helper
61	// so static analysis (TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage) can guarantee no
62	// unbounded text egress on the MCP surface. FEAT-022 §10 / Stage E1.
63	func mcpText(text string) mcpContent {
64		clamped, truncated, originalBytes := evidence.ClampOutput(text, serverInlineCapBytes)
65		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="single mcpContent{Type:\"text\"} literal site for the package; Text is sourced from evidence.ClampOutput above (FEAT-022 §10 Stage E1)"
66		return mcpContent{Type: "text", Text: clamped, Truncated: truncated, OriginalBytes: originalBytes}
67	}

90	// New creates a new DDx server bound to addr, serving data from workingDir.
91	func New(addr, workingDir string) *Server {
92		nodeName := resolveNodeName()
93		stateDir := serverAddrDir() // XDG-standard user-level dir, one per node
94		state := loadServerState(stateDir, nodeName)
95		state.workingDir = workingDir
96	
97		workers := NewWorkerManager(workingDir)
98		workers.ReconcileStaleWorkers()
99		beadHub := bead.NewWatcherHub(250 * time.Millisecond)
100		s := &Server{
101			Addr:       addr,
102			WorkingDir: workingDir,
103			mux:        http.NewServeMux(),
104			startTime:  time.Now().UTC(),
105			workers:    workers,
106			beadHub:    beadHub,
107			state:      state,
108		}
109		state.coordinatorReg = workers.LandCoordinators
110	
111		// Register the server's own project immediately.
112		state.RegisterProject(workingDir)
113		_ = state.save()
114	
115		s.routes()
116		return s
117	}

2639	func (s *Server) handleMCP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
2640		var req jsonRPCRequest
2641		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
2642			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, jsonRPCResponse{
2643				JSONRPC: "2.0",
2644				ID:      nil,
2645				Error:   &rpcError{Code: -32700, Message: "parse error"},
2646			})
2647			return
2648		}
2649	
2650		var resp jsonRPCResponse
2651		resp.JSONRPC = "2.0"
2652		resp.ID = req.ID
2653	
2654		switch req.Method {
2655		case "initialize":
2656			resp.Result = map[string]any{
2657				"protocolVersion": "2025-03-26",
2658				"capabilities": map[string]any{
2659					"tools": map[string]any{},
2660				},
2661				"serverInfo": map[string]any{
2662					"name":    "ddx-server",
2663					"version": "0.1.0",
2664				},
2665			}
2666		case "tools/list":
2667			resp.Result = map[string]any{
2668				"tools": s.mcpTools(),
2669			}
2670		case "tools/call":
2671			resp.Result = s.mcpCallTool(req.Params, r)
2672		case "notifications/initialized":
2673			resp.Result = map[string]any{}
2674		default:
2675			resp.Error = &rpcError{Code: -32601, Message: "method not found"}
2676		}
2677	
2678		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, resp)
2679	}

Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

## DDx Repository Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the DDx repository to understand HTTP routes, MCP tools, agent CLI commands, and data structures. Here are my findings:

---

### 1. HTTP Route Registration Pattern

**Location:** `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-4c9f51f9-20260429T192729-eeef7323/cli/internal/server/server.go` (lines 555-752)

**Pattern for Adding Routes:**

Routes are registered through a simple wrapper pattern. The `Server` struct calls `route()` which appends the pattern to a slice and registers the handler with `http.ServeMux.HandleFunc()`:

```go
func (s *Server) route(pattern string, handler http.HandlerFunc) {
    s.routePatterns = append(s.routePatterns, pattern)
    s.mux.HandleFunc(pattern, handler)
}
```

**Handler Structure:**

Routes are defined in the `routes()` method and follow a naming convention `handle<Name>`. Handlers have the signature:
```go
func (s *Server) handle<Name>(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

**Middleware Pattern:**

Routes support trust gating via `requireTrusted()` middleware, and project scoping via `projectScoped()` and `singletonScoped()`:

- **Unscoped trusted routes:** `trusted(pattern, handler)`
- **Project-scoped routes:** `scoped(pattern, handler)`
- **Legacy singleton routes:** `legacy(pattern, handler)` - auto-resolves to single registered project

**Example routes registered:**
- `GET /api/health` - health check (no trust gate)
- `GET /api/ready` - readiness check (no trust gate)
- `GET /api/projects` - list projects
- `POST /api/projects/register` - register a project
- `POST /api/agent/run` - dispatch agent
- `GET /api/beads/{id}` - show bead
- `POST /mcp` - MCP protocol handler

---

### 2. MCP Tools Pattern

**Location:** `/cli/internal/server/server.go` (lines 2681-3301)

**Tool Definition Pattern:**

Tools are defined in `mcpTools()` method which returns a slice of `mcpTool` structs:

```go
type mcpTool struct {
    Name        string
    Description string
    InputSchema map[string]any  // JSON schema for tool parameters
}
```

**Tool Registration Example:**
```go
{
    Name: "ddx_list_documents",
    Description: "List documents in the DDx library",
    InputSchema: map[string]any{
        "type": "object",
        "properties": map[string]any{
            "project": projectProp,  // Optional project arg
        },
    },
}
```

**Tool Call Handling:**

The `mcpCallTool()` method (line 3126) is the dispatcher:
1. Unmarshals the JSON-RPC call parameters
2. Extracts the tool name and arguments from the call
3. Routes to specific implementation method (e.g., `mcpListDocuments()`, `mcpBeadCreate()`)
4. Returns `mcpToolResult` with content and error flag

**MCP Protocol:**

The `/mcp` endpoint implements JSON-RPC 2.0 with support for:
- `initialize` - returns protocol version, capabilities, server info
- `tools/list` - returns available tools
- `tools/call` - executes a tool with arguments
- `notifications/initialized` - initialization notification

**Key MCP Tools:**

Read-only tools (sample):
- `ddx_list_documents`, `ddx_read_document`, `ddx_search`
- `ddx_list_beads`, `ddx_show_bead`, `ddx_bead_ready`, `ddx_bead_blocked`
- `ddx_agent_sessions`, `ddx_provider_list`, `ddx_provider_show`

Write/dispatch tools (require trust gate):
- `ddx_bead_create`, `ddx_bead_update`, `ddx_bead_claim`
- `ddx_agent_dispatch`, `ddx_exec_dispatch`

---

### 3. Agent CLI Commands

**Location:** `/cli/cmd/` directory

**Main agent command:** `newAgentCommand()` in `agent_cmd.go` (lines 63-122)

**Subcommands registered:**
- `ddx agent run` - invoke an agent
- `ddx agent list` - list harnesses
- `ddx agent capabilities [harness]` - show agent capabilities
- `ddx agent models` - list models for configured provider
- `ddx agent providers` - list configured providers
- `ddx agent usage` - show per-harness token and cost summary
- `ddx agent catalog show` - show model catalog
- `ddx agent doctor` - check agent harness health
- `ddx agent log` - view session logs
- `ddx agent execute-bead` - execute work items
- `ddx agent execute-loop` - execute in a loop with escalation

---

### 4. Data Structures for Models/Catalog/Capabilities

**Location:** `/cli/internal/agent/` directory

#### **Model Catalog YAML Structure**

**File:** `/cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go`

```go
type ModelCatalogYAML struct {
    Version   int

```

**Built-in defaults (3 tiers):**
- `smart` - Hard/broad tasks, user interactive sessions
- `standard` - Default for most builds (refactoring, feature work)
- `cheap` - Mechanical tasks (extraction, formatting)

Each tier maps surfaces (codex, claude, embedded-openai, etc.) to models.

### 5. Agent CLI Command Implementations

#### **`ddx agent models`**
**File:** `/cli/cmd/agent_models.go`

**Returns:** List of models for a provider with:
- Model ID
- Rank position (auto-selection order)
- Catalog reference (if recognized)
- Configured model marker (*)
- Auto-selected marker (>)

**Flags:**
- `--provider` - filter by provider name
- `--all` - list all providers' models

#### **`ddx agent catalog show`**
**File:** `/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (lines 1338-1436)

**Returns:**
- Tier definitions with surface→model mappings
- Blocked models list
- Reachability warnings (probes service to verify catalog models resolve to healthy routes)

**Flags:**
- `--project` - project root for probe verification

#### **`ddx agent capabilities [harness]`**
**File:** `/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (lines 592-684)

**Returns:**
- Harness name and binary path
- Configured model (with source: default or config override)
- Available models list
- Reasoning levels
- Profile mappings (smart → model, fast → model, etc.)
- Config example YAML

**Flags:**
- `--harness` - explicit harness name
- `--json` - JSON output

#### **`ddx agent usage`**
**File:** `/cli/cmd/agent_usage.go`

**Returns:** Per-harness aggregated stats:
```go
type usageRow struct {
    Harness              string
    Sessions             int
    InputTokens          int
    OutputTokens         int
    CostUSD              float64
    CostBasis            string  // reported, estimated, estimated_value, mixed
    AvgDurationMS        float64
    QuotaState           string  // ok, blocked, unknown
    SignalProvider       string  // source of routing signal
    SignalKind           string
    SignalFreshness      string
    NativeInputTokens    int     // from upstream provider
    NativeOutputTokens   int
    NativeTotalTokens    int
    NativeSessionCount   int
    NativeQuotaUsedPercent int
}
```

**Flags:**
- `--harness` - filter by harness name
- `--since` - time window (today, 7d, 30d, ISO date)
- `--format` - table, json, csv

**Data sources (priority):**
1. Routing metrics store (DDx-observed routing outcomes)
2. Session index (legacy fallback)
3. Enriched with upstream routing signals (quota, usage, provider info)

#### **`ddx agent providers`**
**File:** `/cli/cmd/agent_providers.go`

**Returns:** Per-provider status:
```go
type providerStatusEntry struct {
    Name          string  // e.g., "claude", "codex"
    Type          string  // "anthropic", "openai"
    BaseURL       string
    Model         string  // default model
    Default       bool
    Status        string  // live connectivity status
    CooldownUntil string  // if on routing cooldown
}
```

**Flags:**
- `--json` - JSON output

### Key Design Patterns

1. **Route Registration:** Simple wrapper over `http.ServeMux` with trust-gating middleware and optional project scoping
2. **MCP Tools:** JSON-RPC 2.0 dispatcher with tool definitions as data (name, description, schema)
3. **CLI Commands:** Cobra-based with CommandFactory receiver, flags for filtering/format control
4. **Data Structures:** Type-safe YAML/JSON serializable structs with clear responsibility (SessionEntry for logging, HarnessCapabilities for introspection, ModelCatalogYAML for tier mappings)
5. **Agent Service:** Abstraction layer over provider endpoints with fallback to legacy global config, supports multi-project scoping

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)

go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
          fi
        done

Committed. The bead is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:

**New file**: `cli/internal/server/agent_catalog.go`
- `handleAgentModels` — serves `GET /api/agent/models`, supports `?provider=` and `?all=true` query params
- `handleAgentCatalog` — serves `GET /api/agent/catalog`, returns effective model catalog (file or built-in)
- `handleAgentCapabilities` — serves `GET /api/agent/capabilities`, supports `?harness=` query param
- MCP implementations: `mcpAgentModels`, `mcpAgentCatalog`, `mcpAgentCapabilities`

**Updated** `server.go`:
- 6 new routes: 3 legacy + 3 project-scoped variants under `/api/projects/{project}/agent/`
- 3 new MCP tool definitions in `mcpTools()`: `ddx_agent_models`, `ddx_agent_catalog`, `ddx_agent_capabilities`
- 3 new cases in `mcpCallTool()` dispatcher

**Updated** `server_test.go`: tool count updated from 37 → 40 with the 3 new tools added to the expected list. All server tests pass.
